title: bridge-jmap SSRF guard and HTTPS enforcement on apiUrl is well-designed
description: init_session() validates: (1) apiUrl must be https://, (2) apiUrl origin must exactly match the session URL origin. This prevents a malicious JMAP server from redirecting the bearer token to an internal host (AWS metadata, internal APIs). The same-origin check uses url::Url::origin() which normalises scheme and host before comparison. A contributor might simplify this to a prefix check, which would miss path-traversal attacks. The current approach is correct and should be preserved.
